question.text: The AIF Manager shall be responsible for:

answer.text: Only the investment decisions of the fund
Every decision of the Category I/II AIF

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per SEBI (AIF) Regulations, the Manager shall be responsible for every decision of the Category I AIF/Category II AIF. This places comprehensive accountability on the manager. **The Trap:** The responsibility is not limited to investment decisions — it covers all fund decisions including compliance, reporting, and operational matters. **Cross-Exam Context:** Manager accountability is a core principle tested in XIX-A and XIX-C. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The comprehensive responsibility of the manager means thorough due diligence on the management team is critical. Evaluate not just their investment track record but their operational capabilities, compliance infrastructure, and governance standards.
